title: Festuca oelandica
intro: Festuca oelandica, also known as Oland grass or sea fescue, is a species of grass native to the coastal areas of Europe and North America. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Festuca oelandica include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Festuca oelandica is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as dried and powdered forms, which are used to treat respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Festuca oelandica is classified within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Festuca, which includes various types of fescue grasses. The species name oelandica is derived from Öland, a Swedish island where the plant is native. Within the genus Festuca, F. oelandica is often grouped together with other species such as F. rubra and F. altaica. This classification helps to understand the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics.
